analysis_result: LZTR1 mutation frequency in benign/atypical NF WES cohort: 3/36 = 8.3% (3 samples with LZTR1 mutations: Tumor_1083 [frame shift del p.Q10Rfs*15], Tumor_1799 [missense p.R340Q], Tumor_1901 [missense p.R340Q]; denominator = 16 Neurofibroma + 20 Atypical_NF WES samples). If denominator is restricted to the 32 benign/atypical samples present in the mutation file, frequency = 3/32 = 9.4%.
denominator_used: N = 36; benign/atypical NF samples with WES data (Tumor Type in {Neurofibroma, Atypical_NF}, WES column = 'X' in Clinical Data sheet of Supplementary Data 1/MOESM4). Includes 16 Neurofibroma and 20 Atypical_NF samples across methylation groups PremalignantNF_G3 (n=24), Benign_NF-G4 (n=3), and Sporadic Spinal NF_G5 (n=9).
assumptions_made: Benign NF oncoprint assumed to refer to benign/atypical neurofibroma WES cohort (i.e., all non-MPNST samples with WES), excluding MPNST and Low-Grade MPNST samples; 'mutation' defined as any variant called in Supplementary Table 2 (MOESM5) with no filtering by silent vs nonsilent, variant class, or oncogenicity; 4 benign/atypical samples (Tumor_2138, Tumor_2178, Tumor_882, Tumor_2414) had no mutations listed in the table and are treated as LZTR1-wildtype (i.e., included in denominator but not numerator); denominator of 36 matches the total benign/atypical WES cohort reported in Clinical Data.
step_by_step_analysis:
A. DATA LOADED
- Clinical Data: 41467_2023_38432_MOESM4_ESM.xlsx, sheet 'Clinical Data', 108 rows x 15 cols. Relevant columns: Tumor_ID, Tumor Type, Methylation_Group, WES, WES label.
- All WES mutations: 41467_2023_38432_MOESM5_ESM.xlsx, sheet 'Supplementary Table 2', 7457 rows x 123 cols. Relevant columns: Tumor_Sample_Barcode, Hugo_Symbol, Variant_Classification, HGVSp_Short.

B. COHORT DEFINITION
- Filter 1: WES == 'X' -> 54 samples with WES data
- Filter 2: Tumor Type in {Neurofibroma, Atypical_NF} (benign/atypical NF only; excludes MPNST and Low-Grade MPNST) -> 36 samples
  - 16 Neurofibroma
  - 20 Atypical_NF
- Final analytic cohort: 36 benign/atypical NF WES samples

C. VARIABLE CODING
- Sample barcode mapping: Tumor_Sample_Barcode constructed as 'Tumor_' + WES label (to match MOESM5 barcodes).
- LZTR1 defined as Hugo_Symbol == 'LZTR1'. All variant classifications counted (i.e., a sample is 'LZTR1-mutated' if it has >=1 mutation in LZTR1 of any class).
- 32/36 benign/atypical samples appear in the MOESM5 MAF; 4 (Tumor_2138, Tumor_2178, Tumor_882, Tumor_2414) have no mutations in the file. Standard oncoprint convention is to include all analyzed samples in the denominator (so these count as LZTR1-wildtype).

D. COMPUTATION
- Filter MOESM5 to Tumor_Sample_Barcodes in benign/atypical WES cohort: 4428 mutations.
- Filter to Hugo_Symbol == 'LZTR1': 3 mutation records.
  - Tumor_1083 : LZTR1 Frame_Shift_Del p.Q10Rfs*15 (FILTER=PASS, TGL_FILTER_VERDICT=PASS)
  - Tumor_1799 : LZTR1 Missense_Mutation p.R340Q (PASS, PASS)
  - Tumor_1901 : LZTR1 Missense_Mutation p.R340Q (PASS, PASS)
- Unique LZTR1-mutated samples: 3.
- Denominator = 36 (all benign/atypical NF WES samples).
- Frequency = 3 / 36 = 0.0833 = 8.3%.

E. RESULT
- LZTR1 mutation frequency in benign/atypical NF oncoprint = 3/36 (8.3%).
- Alternate denominator (only samples appearing in MAF): 3/32 = 9.4%.

F. CAVEATS
- Supplementary Figure 3a is not viewable in blinded mode; denominator interpretation inferred from clinical data and standard oncoprint conventions.
- If the published oncoprint uses only Neurofibroma samples (excluding Atypical_NF), numerator/denominator would differ: 1/16 = 6.3% (only Tumor_1083 is Neurofibroma; Tumor_1799 and Tumor_1901 are Atypical_NF).
- If the published oncoprint includes Low-Grade MPNST as 'benign/atypical NF', denominator = 39 and frequency = 3/39 = 7.7%.
- No mutation filtering applied (includes silent + nonsilent); all 3 LZTR1 mutations are nonsilent (1 frame shift del, 2 missense), so result is unchanged if restricted to nonsilent.
